Schedule, manage and publish social medi: Schedule, manage and publish social media content with FeedHive.
Creates a new post in FeedHive (draft or: Creates a new post in FeedHive (draft or scheduled).
The main content of your post.
Choose \"Draft\" to save without schedul: Choose "Draft" to save without scheduling, or "Scheduled" to set a publish date.
Required when Status is \"Scheduled\". T: Required when Status is "Scheduled". The exact date and time the post should be published.
Select the social media accounts to publ: Select the social media accounts to publish this post to.
Optionally assign labels to organise thi: Optionally assign labels to organise this post in FeedHive.
Private notes visible only to your team: Private notes visible only to your team in FeedHive. Not published.
Automatically shorten URLs in your post: Automatically shorten URLs in your post text.
Updates an existing post in FeedHive. On: Updates an existing post in FeedHive. Only the fields you fill in will be changed.
Select the post you want to act on.
The new content of the post. Leave empty: The new content of the post. Leave empty to keep the current text.
Change the post status. Leave empty to k: Change the post status. Leave empty to keep the current status.
Update the scheduled publish date and ti: Update the scheduled publish date and time.
Update the private notes for this post.
Automatically shorten URLs in the post t: Automatically shorten URLs in the post text.
Retrieves the details of a specific post: Retrieves the details of a specific post.
Deletes a post from FeedHive. This actio: Deletes a post from FeedHive. This action cannot be undone.
Returns a list of posts, optionally filt: Returns a list of posts, optionally filtered by status or labels.
Only return posts with this status. Leav: Only return posts with this status. Leave empty to return all posts.
Maximum number of posts to return. Betwe: Maximum number of posts to return. Between 1 and 100.
Creates a new label in FeedHive for orga: Creates a new label in FeedHive for organising posts.
The name of the label (e.g. \"Campaign Q: The name of the label (e.g. "Campaign Q1", "Evergreen Content").
Fires a FeedHive Workflow Trigger to cre: Fires a FeedHive Workflow Trigger to create a post. The workflow defines which accounts to post to, the AI model to use, and any default content.
The ID of your FeedHive workflow trigger: The ID of your FeedHive workflow trigger. Found in FeedHive → Workflows → your workflow settings.
The content of the post. Leave empty to: The content of the post. Leave empty to let your workflow generate or use a template.
Ask FeedHive AI to generate the post con: Ask FeedHive AI to generate the post content. Only used if your workflow is configured for AI generation.
When to publish the post. Leave empty to: When to publish the post. Leave empty to use the schedule defined in the workflow.
Public URLs of images or videos to inclu: Public URLs of images or videos to include in the post (e.g. https://example.com/photo.jpg).
Title: Title
Post title — only applicable for YouTube: Post title — only applicable for YouTube, Google My Business, and Pinterest posts.
A URL to attach to the post — only appli: A URL to attach to the post — only applicable for YouTube, Google My Business, and Pinterest posts.
If your workflow template uses placehold: If your workflow template uses placeholders like [[first_name]], provide them here as key-value pairs (e.g. first_name → John). Each key maps directly to a [[placeholder]] in your template.
Triggers when FeedHive sends a notificat: Triggers when FeedHive sends a notification (post published, scheduled, failed, or comment added).
Choose the event type you will configure: Choose the event type you will configure in FeedHive. This is used to load sample data when testing the trigger — it does not filter incoming webhooks.
